Ezra gathered the exiles of Israel and the dispersed people of Judah. God raised up the spirits of a few remaining people to accompany him. It is regrettable that good people should neglect a good work simply because they were not asked.

Ezra arranged for Levites to go with him; but what difference will that make unless he has God with him? Those who seek God are safe under his protection, even in their greatest dangers; but those who abandon him are always exposed. When starting a new phase of life, we should be careful not to bring any guilt from our past into it. When we are in danger, let us be at peace with God, and then nothing can truly harm us. We should commit all our concerns about ourselves, our families, and our possessions to God through prayer, and leave their care with him. And sometimes, we should decline opportunities that are available to us, so as not to cause others to stumble, and thus dishonor our God. Let us ask wisdom from God, so that we may know how to use or refuse lawful things. We will experience no loss by risking, suffering, or giving up for the Lord’s sake. Their prayers were answered, and the outcome showed it. Those who have earnestly sought God have never found they sought him in vain. In times of difficulty and danger, setting aside time for private or group prayer is the best way we can find relief.

If we expect God to protect what belongs to us through his providence, let us, by his grace, protect what belongs to him. Let God’s honor and interest be our concern; then we may expect that our lives and comforts will be his.

Enemies plotted against the Jews, but God protected them. Even the usual dangers of travel call for us to go out in prayer and return with praise and thanks. But what shall we give in return when the Lord has safely led us through life’s journey, through the dark valley of death, away from all our enemies, into eternal happiness! Among their sacrifices they had a sin offering. Atonement sweetens and secures every blessing to us, which will not be truly comforting unless sin is removed, and we are at peace with God. Then the church rested. The expressions used here point us to the deliverance of sinners from spiritual bondage and their journey to the heavenly Jerusalem under the care and protection of their God and Savior.
